title: Can someone calculate how strong Ramiels beam is
display_title: Can someone calculate how strong Ramiels beam is
wikitext:
[]: Can someone calculate how strong Ramiel's beam is?             Quote Post by solarghi12 » Sun Jun 16, 2024 2:59 pm Hi!!! I am really trying to figure out how strong Ramiel's beam is in the rebuilds,( particularly in terajoules) and I was wondering if anyone could calculate this for me. I believe someone tried before but it was a high/lowball and it was a while ago. Thanks!

[]: Re: Can someone calculate how strong Ramiel's beam is?             Quote Post by nerv bae » Mon Jun 17, 2024 9:05 am I asked a generative AI how much energy it would take to vaporize Mount Fuji, and got the following output: Given: - The mass of Mount Fuji is estimated to be around 5.9 x 10^12 kg. - The rock composition of Mount Fuji is primarily andesite, with a specific latent heat of vaporization of around 7,500 kJ/kg (a reasonable approximation). Total energy = Mass of Mount Fuji × Specific latent heat of vaporization Total energy = (5.9 x 10^12 kg) × (7,500 kJ/kg) Total energy = 4.425 x 10^16 kJ Total energy = 4.425 x 10^13 TJ (terajoules) Therefore, the approximate amount of energy required to vaporize the entirety of Mount Fuji would be around 4.425 x 10^13 terajoules (TJ) or 44.25 quadrillion terajoules.
